title: Coleus parishii
taxonomy: Coleus parishii is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to the island of Madagascar and is known for its unique and vibrant foliage. The plant is often used as an ornamental in tropical and subtropical gardens. Coleus parishii is a member of the genus Coleus, which is a group of plants that are often referred to as "Coleus" or "Painted Nettles". The classification of Coleus parishii is further supported by its inclusion in the APG III system, which is a widely accepted classification system for plants.
uses: Coleus parishii is known to have several medicinal uses in traditional medicine, including its application in treating fever and inflammation. The plant has been used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues.
